19.reason: The loop condition is incorrect; it should be i < q to shift elements right, but this loop only runs once (i=p to i=p), failing to restore the array properly.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition is incorrect; it should be i < q to shift elements right, but this loop only runs once (i=p to i=p), failing to restore the array properly.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition is incorrect; it should be i < q to shift elements right, but the current loop only runs once (i=p to i=p), failing to restore the array properly.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  The loop should run from p to q-1 to shift all elements left, not just i=p. It should be i < q.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Loop runs only once; should shift elements left from p to q-1 (i.e., for(int i=p; i<q; i++)).
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition `i<=p` only executes once, failing to shift elements back correctly. It should be `i<q` to restore the array state after the recursive call.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The loop condition was incorrectly set to i<=p, which only executes once or not at all depending on p. It should shift elements from p+1 to q back to the left, so the loop should run from p to q-1.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The loop runs only once (i=p to i=p), but it should shift elements from p to q-1 leftward to restore the original array. The loop condition should be i < q, not i <= p.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The loop runs only once (i from p to p), but it should shift elements from p to q-1 leftward to restore the original array. This causes incorrect restoration of the array state during backtracking, leading to wrong permutations.]

9.reason: The loop should stop before p to avoid accessing num[p-1] (out-of-bounds when p=0). It should be i > p.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  The loop should stop before p to avoid accessing num[p-1] (out-of-bounds when p=0). It should be i > p.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Loop condition and direction are wrong; should shift elements from q down to p+1, not including p. This causes out-of-bounds access when i==p (num[p-1]).
